rulings.Case Title: EDITH WONG VS. SUET YIM DIANA LAU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 24, 2011, line 2. CROSS DEFENDANT JEFFREY LEE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Cross-Defendant Lee¿s Motion for Summary Judgment/Summary Adjudication is granted. Cross-Defendant has shifted the burden to show that there was no contract between him and Cross-Plaintiff Diana Lau and that Cross-Defendant never promised to pay rent. In opposition, Cross-Plaintiff relies solely on alleged admissions made in Lee¿s Answer to the UD Complaint. However, Lee never admitted in his Answer that he was a tenant since 2005 or that Cross-Plaintiff was entitled to rent. To the contrary, Cross-Defendant answered that there was no oral agreement between the parties to pay rent and that Cross-Plaintiff told them they could live in the apartment rent free. Because the Breach of Contract fails, the common count claim based on the alleged breach of Contract also fails. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: EDITH WONG VS. SUET YIM DIANA LAU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 24, 2011, line 2. DEFENDANT SUET LAU, YUN-FAI LAU MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendants¿ Diana and Chris Lau¿s Motion for Summary Adjudication to the Complaint and Complaint in Intervention is denied. There is a triable issue of fact as to whether the consideration for the Promissory Note was ever paid. If the consideration was never paid, the Note would be void and the foreclosure improper. Further, even if the $300,000 was paid, Defendant Diana Lau¿s claims that Plaintiff defaulted by not paying her property taxes, not by failing to pay back the $300,000. Whether failing to pay the property taxes was a breach of the Note and Deed of Trust is also disputed. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: NIMER A. MASSIS VS. AURORA LOAN SERVICES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Nimer A. Massis' Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 24, 2011, line 10. Plaintiff Nimer A. Massis' Order To Show Cause To Defendants Re: Preliminary Injunction. Plaintiff's request for a preliminary injunction is denied. Plaintiff cannot prevail on the merits of his claim based on C.C. 2923.5 because the subject property is not plaintiff's primary residence. Moreover, there has not been a showing that this property is "unique" enough for plaintiff to show irreparable harm. Finally, plaintiff cannot post a sufficient bond. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: NORMAN GONOR, INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF A VS. CRAIGSLIST, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, August 24, 2011, line 11. DEFENDANT CRAIGSLIST, INC. DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant's demurrer to plaintiff's complaint is sustained without leave to amend. C.C. 1747.08 on its face does not apply to online transactions. Even if it did not, an analysis under case law, legislative intent and public policy suggest that such transactions are not, and should not be, encompassed by the Act. =(302/LMG)
